obisrecipes 0.0.0.9000

    • Static Maps

Dynamic Maps

dynamic-maps.Rmd
library(obisrecipes)

The dynamic-map functions produce interactive WebGL maps (via mapgl) that render in the RStudio viewer, R Markdown documents, and Shiny apps. The workflow mirrors the static-map approach:

  1. Create a basemap with blank_map(), grey_map(), or carto_map().
  2. Add species occurrence data with add_species_data().
  3. Optionally add UI controls with add_map_controls().

Note: All examples in this vignette require an internet connection because the map tiles and OBIS occurrence data are fetched at render time.

Basemaps

Blank white basemap 

blank_map()

Switch to a flat Mercator projection:

blank_map(projection = "mercator")

Grey OBIS-style basemap

grey_map() matches the style used on OBIS taxon pages: grey ocean, white land, optional GEBCO bathymetry overlay.

grey_map()
grey_map(add_bathymetry = FALSE)

Carto basemaps 

carto_map()
carto_map(carto_map = "dark-matter")

Species occurrence layer

add_species_data() fetches OBIS occurrence tiles for a taxon and adds a colour-scaled fill layer to the map.

Default species (taxonid 126436) on a blank map:

blank_map() |> add_species_data()

Custom species with custom breaks and a legend:

grey_map() |>
    add_species_data(
        taxonid = 955271,
        breaks  = c(0, 5, 50, 500, 5000),
        legend  = TRUE
    )

Fit the map view to the species extent after adding the layer:

ext <- get_species_extent(taxonid = 955271)

blank_map(projection = "mercator") |>
    add_species_data(taxonid = 955271) |>
    fit_bounds(ext)

Using OBIS palettes

Pass any named OBIS palette via the palette argument and add_species_data() will call obis_pal() internally to generate one colour per break. This works with all palettes from scale_fill_obis_cont() and scale_color_obis_cat().

grey_map() |> add_species_data(taxonid = 126436, palette = "thermal")
grey_map() |> add_species_data(taxonid = 126436, palette = "blues")

Pair fewer breaks with a diverging palette:

blank_map() |>
    add_species_data(
        taxonid = 126436,
        breaks  = c(0, 10, 100, 1000),
        palette = "rdbu",
        legend  = TRUE
    )

obis_pal() can also be called directly to preview colours or pass them to other mapgl layer functions:

obis_pal("thermal", 5)
#> [1] "#042333" "#5580B8" "#CDE6C9" "#F3BA3B" "#B12010"
#> [1] "#042333" "#4a69a9" "#cde6c9" "#f0a120" "#b12010"

obis_pal("rdbu", 7)
#> [1] "#053061" "#3783BB" "#A6CFE3" "#F7F7F7" "#F7B799" "#CA4841" "#67001F"
obis_pal("blues", 4, direction = -1)  # reversed: dark → light
#> [1] "#08306B" "#3786C0" "#ABCFE5" "#F7FBFF"

Map controls

add_map_controls() adds fullscreen, globe-projection toggle, and scale bar controls to any map.

blank_map(projection = "none") |> add_map_controls()

Disable the globe toggle and move the scale bar:

grey_map() |>
    add_map_controls(globe = FALSE, scale_position = "bottom-right")

Inline H3J source (requires duckdb, h3jsr, dplyr)

add_h3j_source_inline() encodes a data frame of H3 cells as a data: URI and registers it as a vector source — no file or tile server needed. Use it with any mapgl layer function (e.g. mapgl::add_fill_layer()).

library(duckdb)
#> Loading required package: DBI
library(h3jsr)
library(dplyr)
#> 
#> Attaching package: 'dplyr'
#> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
#> 
#>     filter, lag
#> The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
#> 
#>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

con <- dbConnect(duckdb())

dbSendQuery(con, "install httpfs; load httpfs;")
#> <duckdb_result d6d30 connection=bf2f0 statement='install httpfs; load httpfs;'>

df <- dbGetQuery(con, "
    SELECT records, cell
    FROM read_parquet('s3://obis-products/speciesgrids/h3_7/*')
    WHERE species = 'Minuca rapax'
")

df <- df |>
    mutate(h3 = h3jsr::get_parent(cell, 3)) |>
    group_by(h3) |>
    summarise(value = sum(records))

blank_map(projection = "mercator") |>
    add_h3j_source_inline("h3source", df = df, h3_col = "h3") |>
    mapgl::add_fill_layer(
        id           = "h3layer",
        source       = "h3source",
        fill_color   = mapgl::interpolate(
            column = "value",
            values = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20),
            stops  = c("#2c7bb6", "#abd9e9", "#ffffbf", "#fdae61", "#d7191c")
        ),
        fill_opacity = 0.8
    ) |>
    mapgl::fit_bounds(get_species_extent(taxonid = 955271))

DeckGL H3 widget — maplibre_h3()

maplibre_h3() creates a self-contained htmlwidget combining a MapLibre GL basemap with a DeckGL H3HexagonLayer. Suitable for species richness, record counts, or any per-H3-cell metric.

# df from the duckdb query above
maplibre_h3(df, center = c(-50, -10), zoom = 2)

Enable 3-D extrusion with extruded = TRUE:

maplibre_h3(df, center = c(-50, -10), zoom = 2, extruded = TRUE, elevation_scale = 1000)

On this page

Developed by Silas Principe, OBIS Secretariat.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
